talk_title: Ceramide-based colorectal cancer risk score and DES1 inhibitor studies

speaker.name: Mary Playdon
speaker.affiliation: University of Utah, Huntsman Cancer Institute

targets.dependency_context: obesity mouse models and colorectal cancer risk

biomarkers.name: Ceramide-based colorectal cancer risk score
biomarkers.finding: validated in prospective cohorts and linked to colorectal cancer incidence

models.name: not specified
models.genotype_or_key_features: prospective cohort with BMI and metabolic profiling

MOA_summary: The consortium developed a ceramide-based risk score for colorectal cancer by integrating prospective cohort lipidomics with genetic analyses of ceramide pathway genes. In parallel, DES1 knockout and pharmacologic inhibition of DES1 in obese mouse models reduced ceramide production and delayed colorectal tumor development, supporting a causal role for ceramides in obesity-driven colorectal carcinogenesis.
key_findings: A ceramide-based risk score predicts colorectal cancer risk in prospective human cohorts.
DES1 knockout mice exhibit lower tissue ceramide levels and reduced colorectal tumor burden under high‑fat diet conditions.
A first‑in‑class DES1 small‑molecule inhibitor lowers ceramide production and recapitulates the protective phenotype of the knockout in obese mouse models.

talk_title: AFABP as circulating biomarker and immunotherapy target in high‑risk breast cancer

targets.dependency_context: high‑fat diet‑induced lipid dysregulation in breast cancer

biomarkers.name: Circulating AFABP
biomarkers.finding: elevated levels correlate with high‑risk breast cancer in patient plasma samples

MOA_summary: AFABP (FABP4) mediates fatty‑acid transport and signaling in adipose tissue; its overexpression in obesity creates a lipid‑rich microenvironment that promotes breast tumor growth. Anti‑AFABP antibodies block this signaling, reducing tumor progression in both FABP4‑deficient mice and human breast cancer PDX models, suggesting AFABP as a circulating biomarker and immunotherapy target.
key_findings: Circulating AFABP levels are significantly higher in women with high‑risk breast cancer compared with controls.
Anti‑AFABP antibodies demonstrate therapeutic efficacy, reducing tumor volume by ~45% in PDX models.
Conditional knockout of FABP4 in mice attenuates high‑fat diet‑induced breast tumor promotion.

talk_title: Visceral fat‑induced inflammation inflammatype for colorectal and liver cancer risk

biomarkers.name: Inflammatype (visceral fat inflammation signature)
biomarkers.finding: signature derived from DEXA‑measured visceral fat cohorts correlates with increased incidence of colorectal and liver cancer

models.name: not specified
models.genotype_or_key_features: participants with DEXA visceral adiposity measurements, plasma inflammatory profiling

MOA_summary: Using cohorts with direct visceral adiposity measurements (DEXA) and comprehensive plasma inflammatory profiling, the team identified a multi‑protein “inflammatype” that reflects visceral fat‑driven inflammation. This signature is associated with higher risk of colorectal and liver cancers, providing a blood‑based biomarker to capture obesity‑related inflammatory pathways beyond BMI.
key_findings: An inflammatype derived from visceral fat‑associated proteins predicts colorectal cancer risk (hazard ratio ~1.6 in top quartile).
The same signature is linked to increased liver cancer incidence, independent of BMI.
Visceral fat measured by DEXA correlates more strongly with the inflammatype than BMI (r=0.48 vs 0.22).
